description: Milestone 4: Discourse private messaging MVP (reply). Enable replying to PM threads with strict safeguards, conservative UX, and robust error handling. Read-only browsing from M3b remains; this milestone adds minimal write action (reply).

userStories.title: Compose UI for replying to an existing PM thread
userStories.description: Add a reply composer to PM thread detail (no new-thread creation yet).
acceptanceCriteria: Message thread detail includes a Reply button that opens a composer
Composer supports plain text only (no attachments, no rich formatting beyond basic markdown passthrough if needed)
Send is disabled while request in flight; clear success/failure states
Unauthenticated users see a login prompt instead of composer
Context7 consulted for current SwiftUI compose patterns + safe text input handling; summary added to progress.txt

userStories.title: Discourse API call for replying to a PM thread
userStories.description: Implement the minimal Discourse API request to post a reply into an existing PM thread.
acceptanceCriteria: Request uses AuthenticatedHTTPClient and applies token refresh rules from M3a
Reply is posted to the correct PM thread and reflected in UI after success (reload thread)
Errors map to domain errors; no crashes; retry available
Context7 consulted for Discourse PM reply endpoint/params; summary added to progress.txt

userStories.title: Add write-action safety rails for messaging
userStories.description: Add conservative safeguards for write actions in an official tool.
acceptanceCriteria: Basic rate limiting: prevent rapid repeated sends (e.g., one send at a time + short cooldown)
Input length constraints and clear validation messages
No logging of message content; logs scrub identifiers
Docs/THREAT_MODEL.md updated for messaging write actions
Context7 consulted for recommended mobile app logging/privacy practices; summary added to progress.txt

userStories.title: Improve PM thread rendering for conversational feel
userStories.description: Refine the PM detail UI for readability and trust.
acceptanceCriteria: Messages show sender name + timestamp (as available) and body excerpt/rendering
Loading/empty/error states are polished and consistent with other tabs
Context7 consulted for SwiftUI list/chat-like layout patterns; summary added to progress.txt
